story.title: Sharon Mann Oral History
story.description: In this interview, Sharon Mann, a resident of Bloomfield, Connecticut, shares her life story and experiences. She discusses her southern roots, moving with her family to Tennessee for a business opportunity, and eventually settling in Bloomfield. Mann describes her education in fine arts and her diverse career trajectory, including retail and garden center management. She and her husband David actively contributed to their community through various initiatives, including forming the Peace Train, theater activities, and a small business focusing on garden detailing. She highlights her involvement with local environmental projects and the Trees for Bloomfield Initiative, aimed at planting trees and fostering community engagement. Mann's Jewish heritage also plays a role in her value system, emphasizing family traditions and giving back. Throughout, she touts the strong community spirit and environmental focus in Bloomfield, advocating for more volunteer involvement to sustain and grow local projects.
